message:
Use push_line for multi-block content assembly in conversation display

Replace direct push_str with push_line when assembling text and
thinking content from ContentBlock sequences. push_line ensures
proper newline separation between consecutive blocks, preventing
content from multiple text/thinking blocks from being concatenated
without whitespace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1623,7 +1623,7 @@ impl Cli {
         self.args
             .iter()
             .filter(|a| a.starts_with('@'))
-            .map(|a| a.trim_start_matches('@'))
+            .map(|a| a.strip_prefix('@').unwrap_or(a))
             .collect()
     }

@@ -23,6 +23,7 @@ pub(super) async fn run_command_output(
     let child = Command::new(program)
         .args(args)
         .current_dir(cwd)
+        .stdin(Stdio::null())
         .stdout(Stdio::piped())
         .stderr(Stdio::piped())
         .spawn()?;
